articles.title: Giuliani’s ‘Doctor’ Reveals How Sick 81-Year-Old Really Was
articles.description: Kylie Cooper/Reuters Rudy Giuliani’s nurse-cum-girlfriend has provided an update on his condition, and says she expects him to make a full recovery. Speaking to Fox News’ Jesse Watters on Monday night, Maria Ryan explained that the former New York City mayor and Trump lawyer began feeling ill after returning from a trip to Paris. Eventually, his breathing deteriorated to the point that he required hospitalization and was placed on a ventilator. While his condition later turned critical, and a Catholic priest was summoned to perform last rites, it has since improved enough that he has been removed from the ventilator and is breathing independently. Giuliani, 81, remains in critical yet stable condition. Read more at The Daily Beast.

articles.title: Amazon One Medical GLP-1 Program Evaluated: How the New Program Works, What It Costs, and What Cash-Pay Patients Should Know First
articles.description: New York City, NY, May 04, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- DISCLAIMER: This article is for informational purposes only and does not constitute medical advice. GLP-1 medications are prescription medications that require evaluation by a licensed clinician. If you purchase through links in this article, a commission may be earned at no additional cost to you. If you searched " Amazon One Medical GLP-1 program " this week, you are not alone. On April 21, 2026, Amazon made one of the most significant moves in the history of consumer healthcare. Its primary care arm, One Medical , launched a national GLP-1 Management Program — combining doctor visits, prescription fulfillment, and same-day pharmacy delivery under one roof. The headlines were immediate. Hims & Hers stock dropped 6%. Walmart rushed to defend its own GLP-1 platform. And millions of Americans who had been watching the weight loss drug market from the sidelines started asking the same question: Is this finally the easy, affordable way to get started on semaglutide or tirzepatide? The honest answer is: it depends on who you are. But for the majority of people currently searching this topic — cash-pay patients without GLP-1 insurance coverage , new patients who need a prescription quickly, and people who live outside the 20-plus cities where One Medical operates — Amazon's program has structural limitations that most coverage of the launch has not addressed clearly. For that patient profile, a telehealth-first alternative called Direct Meds — a LegitScript-certified platform that can issue new GLP-1 prescriptions within 24 to 48 hours, nationwide, without membership fees or in-person visit requirements — may be the faster and more affordable starting point. This guide breaks down exactly how Amazon's GLP-1 program works, what it costs, who qualifies, where it falls short — and what cash-pay patients who don't fit the Amazon model should consider a better alternative instead. What is the Amazon One Medical GLP-1 Program? Amazon has been circling the healthcare space for years. It acquired One Medical in 2023 for $3.9 billion. It built Amazon Pharmacy into one of the largest direct-to-consumer prescription platforms in the country. It launched same-day drug delivery to thousands of cities. Each move felt like a piece of a puzzle being assembled in slow motion. The GLP-1 Management Program , launched April 21, 2026, is what the completed puzzle looks like. For the first time, Amazon is connecting all three of its healthcare assets — One Medical's clinical network, Amazon Pharmacy's fulfillment infrastructure, and its Prime delivery logistics — into a single, end-to-end weight loss program. The result is a product that no pure telehealth company and no traditional pharmacy chain could replicate on their own. How Amazon Combined Primary Care, Pharmacy, and Weight Loss in One Program Most GLP-1 programs on the market today operate in silos. A telehealth company writes your prescription. A separate pharmacy fills it. Your primary care doctor — if you have one — may not even know you started treatment. Nobody is watching the full picture. Amazon's model is structurally different. The GLP-1 Management Program integrates weight loss treatment directly into a patient's primary care relationship at One Medical. That means the same clinician who manages your blood pressure, reviews your lab work, and tracks your metabolic health is also the one overseeing your GLP-1 treatment. The program includes pre-visit health screening, a structured consultation to assess eligibility, ongoing follow-up appointments, integrated lab monitoring, and standardized safety protocols. If a patient's weight loss starts affecting cardiovascular markers or blood sugar, their One Medical provider sees that in real time — not six months later at an annual checkup. Amazon Pharmacy then handles the dispensing and delivery side. Patients can see upfront pricing before checkout, compare insurance versus cash-pay costs side by side, and have their medication delivered — in some cities, the same day they receive their prescription. It is a genuinely integrated model. That is both its greatest strength and, as we will explore later, the source of its most significant limitations for certain patients. How Amazon One Medical Differs From Other Telehealth GLP-1 Providers The GLP-1 telehealth market that existed before Amazon's launch was built primarily around convenience and speed. Companies like Hims & Hers, Ro, Noom Med, and dozens of smaller platforms competed on how quickly they could get a patient from online questionnaire to prescription in hand — often in 24 to 48 hours, with minimal ongoing clinical involvement. Amazon's approach makes a deliberate departure from that model. Rather than positioning itself as the fastest or cheapest entry point, Amazon is positioning One Medical's program around clinical integration and long-term outcomes. The program is explicitly designed to treat obesity as a chronic condition requiring continuous medical supervision, not a one-off prescription event. This distinction matters because it affects who the program is actually suited for. A patient who wants a quick online consultation and a prescription shipped to their door within 48 hours will find the Amazon model slower and more involved than many alternatives. A patient who already sees a One Medical provider, has insurance coverage, and wants their GLP-1 treatment managed as part of their overall health picture will find Amazon's offering genuinely differentiated. Best Alternative to Amazon One Medical GLP-1 Program Amazon's GLP-1 program is well-designed for a specific patient profile: insured patients in One Medical markets who want their weight loss treatment integrated into primary care and are comfortable with a more involved clinical onboarding process. For everyone else — cash-pay patients, patients in cities without One Medical locations, patients who need a new prescription quickly, and patients whose budgets align better with compounded GLP-1 pricing — the market for alternatives is active and credible. Direct Meds is one of the most trending and highly recommended platforms in this alternative tier, and for reasons that align directly with the gaps in Amazon's program. Why Thousands of Cash-Pay Patients Are Looking Beyond Amazon The search volume data tells a clear story. Queries combining " GLP-1 without insurance ," " cheapest GLP-1 program ," " compounded semaglutide alternative ," and " GLP-1 new prescription online " have all increased significantly in the days following Amazon's April 21 launch announcement. This surge reflects a specific patient behavior pattern: consumers read about Amazon's program, get excited about the headline pricing and convenience, investigate more deeply, and then discover that the program either does not serve their geographic location, does not offer the prescription access they need, or does not fit their cash-pay budget at $299 per month for injectables. At that point they search for alternatives. And the alternatives that consistently appear — Direct Meds , MEDVi , TrimRx , Eden Health — represent a telehealth-first model that addresses exactly the gaps Amazon's primary-care-integrated approach leaves open. What is Direct Meds and Why is it Gaining Traction in 2026? Direct Meds is a LegitScript-certified telehealth platform that connects patients with licensed healthcare providers for GLP-1 weight loss prescription evaluation. Its model is structurally different from Amazon's in one fundamental way: it is designed from the ground up as a direct-to-patient telehealth service, without the in-person primary care infrastructure that both enables and constrains Amazon's program. The platform operates a performance-optimized intake and consultation funnel — meaning it is built to move patients from initial health screening to prescription evaluation to medication shipment as efficiently as clinical safety standards allow. For patients who prioritize speed of access and simplicity of process over the comprehensive primary care integration that Amazon offers, that efficiency is a meaningful advantage. Direct Meds offers compounded semaglutide and tirzepatide through licensed compounding pharmacies, giving patients access to GLP-1 treatment at price points below the FDA-approved branded medication floor that Amazon Pharmacy operates within. The platform includes nurse support throughout the treatment period and ships prescriptions within one business day of approval. In 2026's regulatory environment — where the FDA has drawn sharper lines around mass-marketed compounding — Direct Meds maintains LegitScript certification as a signal of compliance with the telehealth and pharmacy standards that distinguish legitimate platforms from the bad actors that drew federal enforcement attention. How Direct Meds Works — From Online Consultation to Doorstep Delivery The Direct Meds patient journey is designed for simplicity and speed without sacrificing clinical legitimacy. A patient begins by completing an online health intake — similar in structure to Amazon's pre-visit screening but optimized for asynchronous review rather than in-person consultation. The intake captures medical history, current medications, weight loss goals, and relevant health conditions. A licensed healthcare provider reviews the intake and conducts a telehealth consultation to evaluate prescription eligibility. Direct Meds' model supports new prescription issuance through this telehealth consultation — a critical distinction from Amazon's renewal-only on-demand pathway. Patients who have never been on GLP-1 treatment can go from intake to prescription decision within 24 to 48 hours. Once a prescription is approved, it is sent to a licensed compounding pharmacy for preparation and shipped directly to the patient's door. Direct Meds states a one-business-day shipping timeline from prescription approval — competitive with Amazon's standard delivery and equivalent to same-day delivery for patients in markets not covered by Amazon Pharmacy's same-day network. Nurse support is included throughout the treatment period for clinical questions and ongoing guidance, providing a layer of ongoing access that helps patients navigate the titration phase without requiring scheduled appointments for every interaction. Direct Meds GLP-1 Pricing vs Amazon One Medical — Full Comparison The pricing comparison between Direct Meds and Amazon One Medical is most relevant for cash-pay patients — the segment for whom Amazon's insured pricing advantage does not apply. Direct Meds reflects a consumer-facing pricing model that positions the platform competitively within the compounded GLP-1 telehealth market. For cash-pay patients comparing total monthly cost, Direct Meds' compounded semaglutide pricing sits below Amazon Pharmacy's $299 per month injectable floor — a meaningful difference when multiplied across a 6 to 12 month treatment course. Beyond the per-month medication cost, the total cost comparison also needs to account for consultation structures. Direct Meds' telehealth consultation model is built into the program rather than billed as a separate per-visit line item — removing the consultation-plus-medication cost stacking that affects Amazon's total monthly spend for new patients. For patients with insurance who can access $25 per month GLP-1 coverage through Amazon Pharmacy, that equation reverses decisively in Amazon's favor. The comparison is most relevant — and most favorable to Direct Meds — for the cash-pay patient segment that represents the majority of GLP-1 seekers in the current market. Visit Direct Meds official pricing page here! Can Direct Meds Write a New GLP-1 Prescription Without an Existing One? Yes — and this is the clearest single differentiator between Direct Meds and the Amazon One Medical on-demand telehealth pathway. Direct Meds' licensed healthcare providers can evaluate a new patient and issue a new GLP-1 prescription through a telehealth consultation. Patients who have never been on GLP-1 treatment, who do not have an existing prescription, and who are not eligible for Amazon's renewal-only on-demand pathway are Direct Meds' core patient population. The new prescription capability through telehealth — available nationwide, without geographic proximity to a physical clinic — addresses precisely the access gap that Amazon's program structure creates for new patients outside major One Medical markets. This does not mean Direct Meds prescribes without clinical judgment. The provider review process includes evaluation of medical history, contraindications, and eligibility criteria consistent with responsible GLP-1 prescribing. But the clinical review happens asynchronously through the telehealth model, enabling a faster path from patient inquiry to prescription decision than Amazon's scheduled consultation model supports for new patients. Is Direct Meds Legitimate? LegitScript Certification and Safety Overview In a GLP-1 telehealth market that attracted significant regulatory scrutiny in early 2026 — with the FDA issuing warning letters to more than 30 platforms for marketing violations — legitimacy verification is a reasonable and important question for any patient evaluating a new provider. Direct Meds holds active LegitScript certification . ... Full story available on Benzinga.com
